finding: `ocman --clean-backups` lists only the backups it WILL delete, so the user cannot tell what SURVIVES (22-of-22? 22-of-100?) before confirming a destructive purge.
proposed_action: List ALL backup items with a red DELETE tag for those past the cutoff and a green KEEP tag for those retained, so the outcome is visible before confirming.

area: no forceful warning when ALL backups will be deleted
finding: If every backup is older than the cutoff, the purge removes 100% of rollback safety with no special warning — the same quiet 'Type yes' prompt as a partial purge.
proposed_action: When zero backups would be kept (and there is >=1 backup), print a prominent red warning ('WARNING: this deletes ALL N backups — no rollback backups will remain') above the confirm prompt.

area: 'older than N days' header can mislead
finding: Header says 'Found 22 backups older than 0.1 days' but with fractional/edge cases the label and the actual cutoff timestamp should be consistent and unambiguous; also 'Created' is really the file mtime, not creation time.
proposed_action: Show the concrete cutoff timestamp in the header (already computed) and relabel 'Created' -> 'Modified' (it is st_mtime). Keep the day count too.

finding: Only backups_to_delete is built; to show KEEP rows the loop must also collect kept backups. Currently the kept set is discarded.
proposed_action: Collect both lists (to_delete, to_keep) in the single pass; sort each; render together (or delete-first then keep).

area: column alignment breaks with ANSI color tags
finding: The current f-string pads names/sizes by raw width; adding colored DELETE/KEEP tags (ANSI escapes) will misalign columns because padding counts the escape bytes.
proposed_action: Pad the plain text fields BEFORE wrapping in color, or place the DELETE/KEEP tag in a fixed leading column padded on its plain label; verify alignment with color on.

area: listing ALL backups could be noisy at scale
finding: With hundreds of backups, printing every KEEP row buries the DELETE rows (the actionable signal).
proposed_action: Always list DELETE rows in full; for KEEP rows, list them but collapse to a summary count when they exceed a threshold (e.g. > 20), or gate full KEEP listing behind -v. Keep default readable.

area: dry-run should show the same all-items view
finding: The improved KEEP/DELETE view is most useful in --dry-run (preview); ensure dry-run renders the same annotated list and the ALL-deleted warning.
proposed_action: Render the annotated list + warning in both dry-run and confirm paths; dry-run just skips the prompt/delete.

area: do not rely on color alone
finding: Red/green tags alone fail for color-blind users / no-color terminals.
proposed_action: Use the words DELETE / KEEP (not just red/green) so the distinction survives without color; color is an enhancement, not the sole signal.
